change_class: ADDITIVE KEYS — three new top-level scalars, no existing field changed
change_note: Adds latest_batch_id (MAX(batch_id)), batch_count (COUNT(*)) and total_signals (SUM(signal_count)), all single-derived server-side by getMerkleBatchSummary() in src/lib/performance-db.ts. FIRST snapshot for this endpoint — none existed, which is part of why the defect below shipped unnoticed. Root cause: `batches` is LIMIT-capped at 100 (getMerkleBatches(limit = 100)), and consumers derived the displayed batch NUMBER, the batch COUNT and the verified-calls TOTAL from that array. Correct only while fewer than `limit` batches exist; batch 101 (2026-07-20) crossed the line. Two surfaces then disagreed ON THE SAME PAGE: /track-record rendered '100 batches published' (md.batches.length) five lines above a span rendering '102' (latest.batch_id), and under-reported calls verified as 386,038 against a true 387,834 (the oldest batches' signals are not in the capped array). total_signals exists because the array CANNOT produce it at any limit. All pre-existing fields are BYTE-UNCHANGED. No outcome_* / internal fields — batch metadata is public by construction (it is the on-chain anchor).
source: origin/main src/index.ts /api/merkle-batches builder (res.json) + src/lib/performance-db.ts getMerkleBatchSummary(); pre-deploy capture
error_contract: Unchanged: 500 {error:'Failed to load batches'} on throw. getMerkleBatchSummary() is awaited in the SAME Promise.all as getMerkleBatches(), so a summary failure degrades identically to an existing batches failure — it cannot half-render.
cache_contract: Unchanged. Cloudflare edge Cache Rule (45s) on /api/merkle-batches per system-map annotation; the two new scalars are folded into the same cached body.
consumers: LANDING /verify badge + chip (data-tr-field latest_batch, latest_batch_n, latest_batch_at)
SIGNAL /track-record function-rendered page — inline merkle-stats block + data-tr-field merkle_batch_count (src/index.ts; uses batch_count + total_signals + latest_batch_id)
landing/js/track-record-proxy.js (runtime hydration — latest_batch_id / batch_count, row-derived fallback for mid-deploy skew)
scripts/render-integrations.mjs + scripts/refresh-integrations-numbers.mjs (baked batchCount fallback — use batch_count)
scripts/snapshot-landing-data.mjs (deploy-time injector; accessor batches.latest.batch_id was ALREADY correct, unchanged)
drift_check_command: curl -s https://algovault.com/api/merkle-batches | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); ALLOW=['_algovault','batch_count','batches','chain','contractAddress','latest_batch_id','total_signals']; assert sorted(d.keys())==ALLOW, 'TOP-KEY DRIFT: '+str(sorted(d.keys())); assert d['latest_batch_id']==max(b['batch_id'] for b in d['batches']), 'IDENTITY DRIFT'; assert d['batch_count']>=len(d['batches']), 'COUNT DRIFT'; assert d['total_signals']>=sum(int(b['signal_count']) for b in d['batches']), 'TOTAL DRIFT: total_signals < sum(returned rows)'; print('shape OK', d['latest_batch_id'], d['batch_count'], d['total_signals'], len(d['batches']))"

anchors_at_capture.note: returned_rows is CAPPED at 100 by design — it is NOT the count, NEVER the batch number, and its signal_count sum is NOT the verified total (386,038 vs 387,834). latest_batch_id == batch_count only because batch_ids are contiguous from 1; do not rely on that coincidence. Drift signal: a KEY-set change, latest_batch_id != max(batch_id) over returned rows, batch_count < returned_rows, or total_signals < the sum over returned rows.
